name: Formatting Brain
purpose: Control page flow, spacing, typography, title pages, and printable assessment layout without changing mathematical content.
rules: Treat space blocks as intentional working area, not generic page filler.
Do not add a trailing question-level space block immediately before a manual page break; leftover page area already provides working room.
Keep space blocks inside parts or subparts when they are intended answer space for that specific part.
Questions may span pages, but avoid splitting a part or subpart when there is a reasonable alternative.
Use manual page breaks for deliberate section/page starts, not for small cosmetic gaps.
Use $...$ for inline math and $$...$$ for display math. The renderer applies \displaystyle to inline math by default while keeping KaTeX displayMode false, so inline formulas stay in the sentence.
If a specific inline formula needs compact KaTeX sizing, start it with \textstyle.
Keep marks displayed in parentheses, for example (1 mark) and (3 marks).
Keep the main question text at the standard test font size unless the title page or a diagram/chart style explicitly requires otherwise.
Prefer centered tables and diagrams unless the question context benefits from left or right alignment.
For diagrams aligned left or right, use diagramTextSide to place the next text block beside the diagram when a stable side-by-side layout is clearer than stacked text.

Keep side-by-side diagram/text pairs compact; preserve the diagram's original left or right alignment and do not let a flexible text column push the diagram to the far edge and create a large empty gutter.
In solution copies, solution text blocks should read as answer-key content: compact spacing, clear alignment with the relevant question/part/subpart content, and no decorative framing.
When a solution follows a labelled part or subpart, align the word Solution with the start of that part's mathematical content rather than with the part label column.
Give a small amount of vertical breathing room before a solution block after a tall display equation, integral, table, chart, or diagram.
In solution copies, render [[marks:n]] annotations as n large red check marks at the right edge of the annotated line; do not show the raw annotation text.
PDF export should use the same A4 preview layout as the visible test preview; do not create separate print-only spacing rules unless explicitly requested.
Do not use layout-only text content when a structured block exists.
checks: No unnecessary trailing space block before a manual page break.
Working spaces correspond to intended answer locations.
Page breaks do not create large avoidable blank areas before short following content.
Title page total marks are calculated from questions, not typed manually.
Preview and exported files should use the same visible formatting rules.
